generalCommentary: Graded 78 by the Sotheby's Grading Commitee in 1992. A copy stolen from actor Nic Cage
Listed variously as "NM" and "VF". Was part of a 25,000 cash/trade deal in 1990. $115,000 trade listed in OS27. Was a 9.2

OS 20 Trade data- $108,000. John Verzyl said that it was for $132,000. Either case its the earliest $100,000 comic, at least in terms of trade value. John did also verify the anecdote about this book. It is "perfect." There's nothing wrong with it. It's also supposedly got pretty nice registration.
Best Existing. The "Dentist's" copy. Grade range is 8.0-9.2. What we do know is that he had the option to have both this and the Church copy in hand and went with this one.
Catalog NM+ Generally ranges from 9.0-9.8. OS 21 lists it as "MT." Changed hands in a cash/trade deal- $74,000 (1990)
Used in a lawsuit by DC against Fox.
Of uncertain origin. Potentially two other books on this list (the FN68 copy or the 8.0 Mastro copy) or potentially a new copy.
Sold raw by Sparkle Ciy on eBay
Signed by Bob Kane to his good friend Robert Crestohl
